steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The word Then shows that the dig proceeds in a set order: first a grid is made, then the site is dug and artifacts are recorded.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is sequence — the passage presents a three-step chronological process: grid made, site dug and artifacts recorded, then artifacts removed and analyzed. The blank introduces the third and final step.
  • Choice A — "For instance,": introduces a specific example of a general claim; the artifact removal step is not an example of anything stated — it is the next action in the process. ✗
  • Choice B — "On the contrary,": signals contradiction; the final step does not contradict the previous steps. ✗
  • Choice C — "Earlier,": signals that what follows happened before what was just described, reversing the order of events. ✗
  • Choice D — "Finally,": signals the concluding step in a sequence, fitting after "Then" to mark the third and last stage of the dig. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
